site.tagline: A people-powered, single-issue campaign building a public mandate for affordable and reliable energy for every Australian household.
site.authorisation: Authorised by Z. Hilton, Coalition for Conservation, Sydney NSW

hero.sub: Sign the petition. Tell Australia's leaders to put families first — before ideology, before politics, before experiments.

petition.h1: Sign it. Send a message no politician can ignore.
petition.lede: Your name joins everyday Australians from every state, every electorate, and every walk of life, demanding leadership that puts affordability first.

takeAction.h1: Signing is the start. Here are two ways to amplify it.
takeAction.lede: Two minutes of your time, repeated by ten thousand Australians, becomes a movement no government can ignore. Pick one — or do both.
takeAction.shareText: 1 in 5 Australians can't afford their power bill. I just signed the petition demanding our leaders put affordability first. Add your name: affordableenergy.org.au

news.lede: Coverage from Australia's leading mastheads and our own press releases. Working journalists, please use the contact details below for same-day comment.

about.h1Main: A people-powered campaign with one job:
about.lede: Affordable Energy Australia is not a think tank or a passive research group. We are a strategic campaign engine purpose-built to bridge the execution gap and give a voice to the 80% of Australians who are concerned about increased energy prices arising from Australia's current plans for the energy transition.

donate.h1: Power the campaign that puts families first.
donate.lede: Pick an amount. We'll send you straight to our secure Stripe donation page — no card details ever touch this site.

theProblem.headline: Electricity prices for households have risen 220 per cent since 2008.

pillars.title: Affordable Bills
pillars.desc: Every household should pay a price they can actually afford. Energy is essential infrastructure — not a luxury, and not a market experiment.

pillars.title: Reliable Power
pillars.desc: Lights on. Factories running. Hospitals operating. A grid Australians can depend on, every hour of every day, in every season.

pillars.title: Common Sense Policy
pillars.desc: Decisions made on engineering and economics, not ideology. A grid planned for outcomes: affordability, reliability, jobs.

voices.name: Kevin, 34
voices.loc: Father of two, Central Coast NSW
voices.quote: Power prices are driving up the cost of everything – fuel, groceries, our mortgage. It's getting harder and harder for young families like us to afford the basics.

voices.name: Nathalia, 28
voices.quote: My students are so worried about there not being enough jobs for them when they graduate. I don't see how we can have a strong economy without affordable power.

news.head: Electricity bills could jump 24pc this year
news.summary: Wholesale and network cost pressures could push household power bills up by as much as 24 per cent this year.
news.body:
Household electricity bills are on track to climb by as much as 24 per cent over the coming year, with wholesale price volatility and rising network charges feeding straight through to retail offers. Analysts warn the increases will land hardest on households that can least absorb them.

It is the clearest sign yet that the affordability crisis is far from over. Add your name to the petition and tell Australia's leaders to put household bills first.

news.head: RBA worried higher energy costs could quickly lift consumer prices
news.summary: The Reserve Bank has flagged that rising energy costs risk feeding rapidly into broader consumer prices.
news.body:
The Reserve Bank of Australia has warned that higher energy costs could quickly pass through into the broader cost of living, putting renewed pressure on inflation and household budgets. The central bank singled out electricity and gas as a key risk to its outlook.

When the RBA is worried about your power bill, it is no longer a fringe concern. Sign the petition and demand energy policy that puts affordability first.

news.head: High energy costs prevent Australia from moving up the value chain
news.summary: Persistently high energy costs are holding Australian industry back from higher-value manufacturing.
news.body:
Persistently high energy costs are preventing Australia from moving up the manufacturing value chain, locking the country into exporting raw materials rather than processing them at home. Energy-intensive industries say they cannot justify new investment while power prices remain among the highest in the developed world.

When industry cannot compete, jobs and capability leave the country. Sign the petition for an energy policy that keeps Australian industry competitive.

news.head: Big swings in Australia's electricity market
news.summary: The ABC examines the volatility rattling Australia's electricity market and what it means for household bills.
news.body:
Australia's electricity market is being buffeted by increasingly large price swings, with periods of negative pricing giving way to extreme spikes within the same day. The ABC reports that this volatility is making it harder for retailers to offer stable, affordable plans to households.

Families cannot budget around a market that lurches from one extreme to another. Add your name to the petition for a grid planned around affordability and reliability.

news.head: Transgrid's $603.7m profit revealed as it attempts to slug NSW households with cost overruns
news.summary: Transgrid posted a $603.7 million profit even as it sought to pass major transmission cost overruns on to NSW households.
news.body:
Transgrid has reported a $603.7 million profit at the same time as it seeks to recover major transmission cost overruns from NSW households through higher network charges. Consumer advocates have questioned why families should foot the bill for project blowouts.

Network costs make up a large share of every power bill. Sign the petition and demand transparency and discipline on the costs being passed to households.

news.head: Data centre surge under pressure as business lobby fights green energy mandate
news.summary: A surge in data-centre demand is colliding with a business-lobby push against a green-energy mandate.
news.body:
A rapid surge in data-centre electricity demand is colliding with a business-lobby campaign against a proposed green-energy mandate, raising fresh questions about who will pay for the new generation and transmission required. Industry groups warn that poorly planned mandates risk driving up costs for every other user.

The energy transition must be planned around affordability and reliability, not ideology. Sign the petition to make that the priority.

team.name: Zoe Hilton
team.role: Media and Operations Director, Coalition for Conservation
team.bio: Zoe is a policy analyst and media commentator with a background in energy policy, government and environmental research. She is passionate about advocating for policy change so that every Australian can access affordable, reliable energy. Zoe has co-authored energy policy papers and featured in videos explaining the flaws in official energy models and why Australia's electricity prices are rising. She previously worked as a science and investment policy advisor in the NSW Government and is currently a Director and Company Secretary of FORM Australia.

team.name: Alex O'Brien
team.bio: Alex has over 15 years of experience in senior roles within the wealth advisory sector, where he has led multidisciplinary teams and advised corporate executives, politicians, businesses, high-net-worth individuals, and family groups. He is the President of Responsible Future Illawarra, a genuine grassroots organisation that opposed the controversial Illawarra Offshore Wind Farm and works closely with other affected communities. Through this role, Alex has helped lead a strong campaign, expose behind-the-scenes dealings, highlight conflicts of interest, and shed light on the true drivers behind large-scale renewable energy projects.
